_comment: Hyperparameters for the type-primary forecast pipeline (predict_2026_types.py / forecast_engine.run_forecast).
_tuning_notes: Full parameter sweep S532: 7056-row grid sweep + 528-row focused blending sweep across 11 historic elections (2008-2022). See data/experiments/backtest_sweep_20260408_235337.csv and focused_blending_sweep_*.csv.
_lam_notes: lam=10.0 optimal via full sweep (S532). Higher lam trusts priors more — with year-adaptive priors closely matching the target cycle, strong prior regularization improves calibration. lam matters less than pbs: within pbs=15, lam=10 vs lam=5 is only +0.001 r.
_mu_notes: mu=1.0 retained. Confirmed irrelevant — identical results across all mu values in full sweep. delta_race underdetermined with sparse state-level polls.
_w_vector_mode_notes: core vs full modes produce identical results until method_reach_profiles gains entries for phone_live, phone_ivr, and unknown methodologies. See Known Tech Debt in CLAUDE.md.

forecast._poll_blend_scale_notes: k in alpha = 1/(1 + n_polls/k). Controls blend between county priors (few polls) and type-projected predictions (many polls). k=15 optimal — dominates k=10 across ALL lam/adaptive combinations (+0.5-1% r). Biggest single lever in the sweep.

poll_weighting._half_life_notes: Exponential decay half-life in days. At 30 days a poll loses half its effective weight. Backtest sweep found half_life_days has zero differentiating effect on historic elections — likely due to sparse temporal poll coverage in backtests. Retained at 30 as a reasonable default for 2026 forward predictions where polls are dense.
poll_weighting._pre_primary_discount_notes: Multiplicative factor applied to n_sample for polls conducted before the primary. Pre-primary polls are less informative about general election matchups. Must be in (0, 1]. 0.5 = pre-primary polls count for half.
poll_weighting._use_pollster_rmse_weights_notes: When true, pollster quality is weighted by empirical RMSE from data/experiments/pollster_accuracy.json (backtest results) rather than Silver Bulletin grades. RMSE weights replace grade-based weights for any pollster found in the accuracy file; grade-based weights remain as fallback for unknown pollsters.
poll_weighting._methodology_weights_notes: Multiplicative n_sample adjustment by survey mode. Phone live-interview polls are empirically most accurate; IVR (robocall) polls have the worst track record. Applied multiplicatively on top of RMSE/Silver Bulletin/grade quality weights. unknown = neutral 1.0. Calibrate from pollster accuracy backtests by methodology group.

fundamentals._notes: Fundamentals model configuration. The fundamentals shift is a structural prior based on presidential approval, GDP, unemployment, and CPI. It is blended with the generic ballot shift to produce the combined national environment adjustment.
fundamentals._weight_notes: fundamentals_weight controls the blend: 0.0 = use only generic ballot (current behavior), 1.0 = use only fundamentals, 0.3 = 30% fundamentals + 70% generic ballot. Start conservative (0.3) because the fundamentals model has only 13 training points and LOO RMSE ~3.9pp.

state_economics._notes: State-level economic adjustment from QCEW (BLS Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages). Modulates the national fundamentals shift by each state's relative employment growth: states with faster-than-national job growth get a smaller in-party penalty. Data source: data/raw/qcew_county.parquet (2020-2023, 3,192 counties).
state_economics._sensitivity_notes: sensitivity controls how much state economic conditions modulate the national shift. At 0.5, a state with +3% relative employment growth gets +1.5pp shift adjustment. Higher values amplify state variation. Start conservative at 0.5.
